event_log.headline: GAME STARTED: Kanata Rangers vs Ennismore Eagles (Game #143)
event_log.detail: We need to get off to a strong start against the Eagles as we begin our quest to win Pool C.

event_log.headline: GAME STARTED: Kincardine Kinucks vs Windsor Wildcats (Game #142)
event_log.detail: We should cheer for Kincardine Kinucks since their win would give us a better chance to win Pool C by potentially weakening Windsor Wildcats' position before our upcoming matchup against them.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kanata Rangers 1 - 0 Ennismore Eagles (in progress)
event_log.detail: If we hold this 1-0 lead against Ennismore Eagles, we put ourselves in a strong position to win Pool C, taking first place in 106 of 214 possible scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kanata Rangers 1 - 1 Ennismore Eagles (in progress)
event_log.detail: A tie here would put us in a strong position to win Pool C, as we'd claim first place in 38 of 225 possible scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kincardine Kinucks 1 - 0 Windsor Wildcats (in progress)
event_log.detail: This result would put us in a strong position to win Pool C, as we would win the pool in 48 of 214 scenarios.

event_log.headline: FINAL: Kanata Rangers 1 - 1 Ennismore Eagles
event_log.detail:
What a gutsy performance by our Rangers! We tied 1-1 with the lower-ranked Ennismore Eagles (#25), and while the system calls this an "upset" since they earned a point against us, we showed real heart in this opening pool game.

Our path to winning Pool C got tougher - we now advance as pool winners in just 38 out of the remaining 243 possible outcome combinations, so we'll need strong performances in our remaining games against Kincardine (#3) and Windsor (#41).

event_log.headline: Score update: Kincardine Kinucks 2 - 0 Windsor Wildcats (in progress)
event_log.detail: This result puts us in a stronger position to win Pool C, as we would win the pool in 12 of 77 scenarios if this score holds.

event_log.headline: FINAL: Kincardine Kinucks 2 - 0 Windsor Wildcats
event_log.detail: This wasn't an upset - higher-ranked Kincardine (#3) beat lower-ranked Windsor (#41) as expected. With this result, we now win our pool in only 12 out of 81 remaining outcome combinations, significantly reducing our chances from the 38 out of 243 we had before this game.

scouting.description: This season we've faced several teams heading to Provincials across 5 tournaments. We swept St. Catharines Brock Jr. Badgers in two meetings (6-0, 2-0). Against the rest... let's just say we're looking for redemption. Of note: Durham West Lightning (#29) is in our quarterfinal opponent's pool (Pool F) -- they beat us 2-0 in November, but they're not the favourite to win their pool. In tournament play, anything can happen!

_narrative:
**Where We Stand**
The situation has changed since our last update - while we remain tied for second with Ennismore at 1 point each, Kincardine has now taken sole control of the pool lead with their 2-0 victory over Windsor, putting the #3-ranked Kinucks at 2 points.

**Our Next Game**
We still face Windsor at 3:45 PM this afternoon as scheduled, but now we're taking on a Wildcats team that just absorbed a shutout loss and will be desperate to get on the board against us.

**Around the Pool**
Earlier we were hoping Windsor could keep it close against Kincardine, but the Kinucks handled business with a clean 2-0 win as expected. At 3:45 PM, Ennismore gets their shot at those same Kinucks in what should be a telling matchup - the Eagles will need to prove their tie with us wasn't a fluke when they face the pool's top team.

_coaches_corner.don_cherry: I'll tell ya, that Kanata Rangers girls team just showed you what heart looks like - coming back to tie it up against Ennismore! Now they got the Windsor Wildcats next and these young ladies are just getting warmed up!
_coaches_corner.talking_points:
**Mindset** — That tie against Ennismore shows we can compete with anyone in this tournament when we play our game. We earned a point and showed great character, now we need to build on that performance and take it up another level against Windsor.

**Discipline** — You've been absolutely perfect so far with zero penalty minutes - that's championship-level discipline. Keep playing hard but smart, and don't let Windsor draw us into any unnecessary scrums or retaliation penalties.

**The Opponent** — Windsor is here for a reason and they're going to bring their best effort knowing we're ranked higher. Expect them to play with nothing to lose and try to catch us off guard early, so we need to be ready from the opening faceoff.

**What We Need** — We're in a tight race for the pool win with only 38 scenarios out of 225 getting us through to quarterfinals. We need to earn points today and control what we can control - play our system, execute our game plan, and let our ranking show on the ice.

_tb_health:
**Tiebreaker Health Analysis:**

We're currently tied with Ennismore on both goal differential (0) and goals against (1), meaning any tie in points with them will likely go to deeper tiebreakers beyond rules iii-iv. Our position is extremely fragile - since we're at GD=0, even losing our final game by just 2 goals would put us at -2 (matching Windsor's current GD), while winning by any margin gives us a positive GD advantage.

Every goal matters critically in our remaining games since we have no cushion on goal differential, and minimizing goals against is equally important given our GA=1 tie with Ennismore.
